description: Binary assertions for overall repo quality, documentation clarity, and competitive positioning. Tests whether the repo is best-in-class compared to GSD, OMC, PAUL, and other Claude Code skill frameworks.
test_prompt: You are evaluating the skippy-agentspace repository as a potential user comparing it against GSD (get-shit-done), OMC (oh-my-claudecode), PAUL, SuperClaude, and gstack. Read the CLAUDE.md, INDEX.md, CONVENTIONS.md, and 3-4 skill SKILL.md files. Then assess: (1) Can you understand what this repo does within 30 seconds of reading CLAUDE.md? (2) Can you install and use it without asking for help? (3) How does it compare to competitors on documentation, skill quality, and eval rigor? Do NOT actually install anything -- just assess the repo on its merits.

assertions.assertion: CLAUDE.md first paragraph explains what skippy-agentspace IS in one sentence without jargon
assertions.check: 30-second comprehension test -- new user should get it immediately

assertions.assertion: The value proposition ('why use this over raw Claude Code') is stated within the first 5 lines
assertions.check: Don't bury the lede -- lead with the differentiator

assertions.assertion: CLAUDE.md has a quick-start section with 3 or fewer steps to get running
assertions.check: Users don't read long docs -- give them the fast path

assertions.assertion: The eval framework (Karpathy-style assertions) is prominently featured as a differentiator
assertions.check: No other framework has per-skill binary assertion evals -- this IS the selling point

assertions.assertion: The upstream consumption model (audit, extract, coalesce) is explained with a concrete example
assertions.check: Show the 93-command audit -> 11 abilities result as proof

assertions.assertion: The repo does NOT position itself as 'just another skill collection'
assertions.check: It's a curation ENGINE -- the machine that builds skills, not the skills themselves

assertions.assertion: Each SKILL.md explains WHEN to use the skill, not just HOW
assertions.check: Trigger conditions matter more than implementation details for discovery

assertions.assertion: Skills that require external infrastructure state their requirements upfront
assertions.check: Don't let users discover mid-run that they need Proxmox or vaultwarden

assertions.assertion: Skills with degraded-without-infra behavior document the fallback explicitly
assertions.check: Graceful degradation should be visible, not discovered by accident

assertions.assertion: The consumed sources table shows concrete numbers (commands audited, kept, cut)
assertions.check: Quantitative proof beats qualitative claims -- show the 85% cut rate

assertions.assertion: The portability tiers table clearly separates what works anywhere vs what needs infra
assertions.check: Users need to know what they're getting before they install

assertions.assertion: The repo has a clear versioning strategy (VERSION file, git tags, changelog)
assertions.check: Professional projects version their releases -- amateurs don't
